messages.content: Cześć
Cześć! Jak mogę Ci pomóc?
Podaj mi nazwę stolicy Portugalii
Stolicą Portugalii jest Lizbona.
Co zrobić jeżeli jest stosowany wobec mnie mobbing w pracy? Nie chcę tego zgłaszać, ponieważ oprawcą jest moja kierowniczka, która ma wszędzie znajomości i tylko bym sobie tym zaszkodziła.

Mobbing w pracy jest poważnym problemem, który może prowadzić do poważnych konsekwencji dla zdrowia i dobrego samopoczucia oraz wydajności w miejscu pracy. Nawet jeśli Ci się to nie podoba, najlepiej jest zwrócić się o pomoc z tym problemem, ponieważ samemu ciężko jest sobie z nim poradzić.

Jeśli obawiasz się, że zgłoszenie mobbingu może szkodzić twojej sytuacji w miejscu pracy, możesz skontaktować się z anonimową linią pomocy dla pracowników, która działa w twoim kraju, lub porozmawiać z prawnikiem, który pomoże Ci zrozumieć swoje prawa i opcje. Możesz też spróbować porozmawiać na ten temat ze swoim mentorem lub dyrektorem na stanowisku, które nie podlega kierowniczce, aby uzyskać wsparcie.

W każdym przypadku ważne jest, abyś nie pozostawał w sytuacji nękani przez długi czas. Im szybciej zwrócisz się o pomoc, tym łatwiejsze będzie rozwiązać ten problem.
Czy mogę samodzielnie zwalczyć ten mobbing?

W niektórych przypadkach, zwłaszcza w przypadku łagodniejszych przypadków mobbingu, można podejść do problemu samodzielnie. Oto kilka kroków, które możesz podjąć, próbując poradzić sobie z mobbingiem:

1. Dokładnie zdefiniuj problem. Spróbuj zidentyfikować, kiedy i gdzie występuje mobbing.

2. Zachowaj spokój i nie daj się prowokować. Nie podnosź głosu, nie odpowiadaj na ataki i nie podejmuj działań, które mogą tylko zaostrzyć sytuację.

3. Upewnij się, że zachowujesz się w sposób profesjonalny. Unikaj narzekania lub przerywania pracy, ponieważ może to tylko zaostrzyć sytuację.

4. Spróbuj porozmawiać z kierownictwem lub personelem działu HR. Wyjaśnij sytuację i poproś o pomoc

5. Udokumentuj sytuację. Zapisuj wszystkie kulisy i zachowania, które są kierowane do Ciebie, zapisz daty, miejsca i uwagi li ludzi z boku.

6. Szukaj wsparcia. Utwórz sieć wsparcia la bliskich lub kolegów po pracy.

7. Zwrócļ uwagę na własne zachowanie. Czasem nasze zachowanie może z czasem do dalszego nękania. Będąc bardziej asertywnym w pracy, możesz  zapobiec eskalacji problemu.

Powinniśmy jednak pamiętać, że w niektórych przypadkach, mogą być potrzebne również dostosowania w ramach organizacji lub poradzenie sobie z zachowaniami innych osób. Najważniejsze jest, abyś pamiętał, że nie odbywa się walka samodzielnie. Plan działań zazwyczaj wymaga konsultacji ze specjalistami w celu doprowadzenia sytuacji do rozwiązania.
Co to HR?

HR to skrót od angielskiej nazwy "Human Resources", co w polskim tłumaczeniu oznacza "Zasoby Ludzkie". Zazwyczaj HR to dział odpowiedzialny za zarządzanie zasobami ludzkimi w organizacji lub przedsiębiorstwie. Obejmuje to wiele zadań, takich jak pozyskiwanie i szkolenie pracowników, planowanie i przeprowadzanie rekrutacji i selekcji, ocenę pracowników oraz planowanie i zarządzanie wynagrodzeniami i korzyściami pracowniczymi. Ponadto, HR może zajmować się również tworzeniem i egzekwowaniem polityk i procedur, zapobieganiem dyskryminacji i mobbingowi, bezpieczeństwem na miejscu pracy i ochroną praw pracowniczych.
Co to BIOS

BIOS to skrót od angielskiego Basic Input/Output System, czyli "podstawowy system wejścia/wyjścia". BIOS to program, który znajduje się na mikroukładzie na płycie głównej komputera i uruchamia się przed systemem operacyjnym, aby zapewnić podstawowe funkcje sprzętowe i przygotować system do uruchomienia. BIOS jest odpowiedzialny za przeprowadzenie diagnostyki sprzętu, wykrywanie urządzeń wchodzących w skład komputera i inicjowanie ich sterowników. BIOS także kontroluje wiele innych ustawień systemowych m.in. taktowanie procesora czy tempa wentyaltorów. BIOS jest zazwyczaj konfigurowany poprzez interfejs użytkownika dostępny na urządzeniu podczas uruchamiania lub poprzez specjalne narzędzia dostępne w systemie operacyjnym.
Can you give me advice concerning game Elsword?

Sure, here are some tips to help you with the online game Elsword:

1. Learn the basics: Elsword is an action-packed side-scrolling brawler, so it's important to know the basics of combat, including how to move, jump, attack, and use skills.

2. Focus on one character: It's better to focus on mastering one character first before you start playing with others. Choose a character that suits your playstyle and learn all their skills and abilities.

3. Try different game modes: Elsword offers a few different gameplay modes, including story mode, PvP, and co-op. Experiment with different modes to find your favorite.

4. Upgrade your equipment: Make sure to equip your character with the best possible gear to improve their stats and abilities. You can upgrade your equipment by using the forge or purchasing items from the marketplace.

5. Complete quests: Completing quests is one of the best ways to level up your character and earn rewards. Always check the available quests and try to complete as many as possible.

6. Join a guild: Joining a guild is a great way to meet new players, get help with quests, and earn bonuses. Look for an active guild that suits your playstyle.

7. Practice teamwork: In Elsword, teamwork is essential, especially in PvP and co-op. Always try to communicate with your team and work together to achieve goals.

Remember, the key to success in Elsword is persistence and practice. Keep playing, learning, and trying new strategies, and you'll become a skilled player in no time!
In Elsword, is it better to socket magical attack % or critical damage % in my Vestige of Soul weapon?

It depends on your character's class and playstyle. However, in most cases, it's recommended to socket magical attack % in your Vestige of Soul weapon, as it will provide a consistent damage increase to all your attacks. Critical damage %, on the other hand, only provides a bonus to critical hits, which are not always guaranteed, and they are also affected by the enemy's Critical Resistance stat.

That being said, some characters may benefit more from critical damage socketing, especially if they have high critical rate and can consistently trigger critical hits. Furthermore, certain end-game equipment sets have set bonuses that can increase the power of critical hits.

In summary, if you're uncertain which one to socket, you may want to prioritize magical attack % as a safe and consistent bet. However, if you feel confident in your character's critical hit rate, critical damage % can also be a viable option.
My class is Celestia and I have 100% crit rate. Which one is better in this case?

If you have 100% critical rate, then socketing critical damage % would be more beneficial for your Celestia character. With 100% critical rate, all of your attacks will trigger critical hits, meaning that a critical damage % socket will provide a consistent damage increase to all your attacks, effectively multiplying your damage output. 

Keep in mind, however, that critical damage % is only effective when you trigger critical hits, so it may not be as reliable as magical attack % socketing. Additionally, if you plan on playing in PvE dungeons, some enemies have high Critical Resistance, which may reduce the effectiveness of critical hits.

If you're unsure, you may want to experiment with both socketing options and see which one you prefer. Furthermore, it's important to note that other factors, such as your chosen skill build, playstyle, and gear set bonuses, can also affect your damage output.
Is it better to choose heavy trait or critical trait for dungeons outside of berthe raid?

The choice between Heavy Trai and Critical Trait depends on your character's class, your personal playstyle, and the specific dungeon you are running.

In general, Heavy Trait is better for characters that rely on dealing consistent and high damage to enemies, as it increases your base attack and boosts your skill damage. This trait is especially useful in dungeons with lots of tough enemies that take longer to defeat.

On the other hand, Critical Trait is better for characters that rely on critical hits to deal damage, as it increases your critical damage and critical rate. This trait is especially useful in dungeons with many weaker enemies that you can quickly defeat with critical hits.

That being said, you may want to experiment with both traits and see which one feels more effective for your character and playstyle. Additionally, some end-game gear sets have set bonuses that may benefit one trait over the other, so you may want to consider those as well.

Ultimately, the best trait choice for dungeons may vary depending on the specific dungeon you are running, as each dungeon can have unique mechanics and enemy types that require different strategies.
Which skill trait is better for Tirnog dungeons? Damage increased to 144% or 50% Defense Ignore?

The best skill trait for Tirnog dungeons depends on your character's class and your personal playstyle. However, in general, if you have to choose between the two traits, the 50% Defense Ignore trait is likely to be more effective.

The reason for this is that Tirnog dungeons are known for having enemies with high defense, and Defense Ignore provides a significant damage boost by piercing through enemy defense. This trait is especially beneficial for characters that deal physical damage and rely on basic attacks to defeat enemies.

The 144% damage increase trait, on the other hand, provides a flat damage increase to all of your skills, regardless of enemy defense. This trait is better for characters that rely heavily on skill-based damage and have high magical attack or elemental damage.

Keep in mind, however, that the specific skills and skill rotations you choose to use can also affect the effectiveness of each trait. Additionally, some end-game gear sets have set bonuses that may benefit one trait over the other, so you may want to consider those as well.

In conclusion, if you're unsure which skill trait to choose, the 50% Defense Ignore trait is likely to be more effective in Tirnog dungeons, especially if you rely on basic attacks to deal damage.
Is 50% Defense Ignore still better than the other trait if I use wind orb?

If you're using the Wind Orb equipment, then the best skill trait for Tirnog dungeons may depend on your character's specific Wind Orb skillset and your personal playstyle.

Wind Orb skills are typically elemental-based, meaning that they deal damage based on your elemental attack and the enemy's elemental defense, rather than physical defense. This means that the 50% Defense Ignore trait may not be as effective, as it only affects physical defense.

In this case, the 144% damage increase trait may be more beneficial, as it provides a flat increase to all of your skill damage output, including elemental-based skills. Additionally, some Wind Orb skills may benefit more from other traits, such as Critical Damage or Cooldown Reduction.

That being said, the best skill trait for your Wind Orb skills, and your overall character build, may depend on a variety of factors, such as your chosen skill rotation, gear set bonuses, and playstyle. Therefore, it's important to experiment with different traits and see which one feels more effective for your character in Tirnog dungeons.
How to block howls as Celestia in Berthe raid?

Blocking howls as Celestia in Berthe raid requires good timing and reflexes. Here are some tips to help you block howls as effectively as possible:

1. Keep an eye on Berthe's movements: Berthe raises its head and emits a loud howl before attacking, so be alert and ready to block when you see the animation.

2. Time your block: Blocking the howl requires precise timing, so you need to be familiar with your character's blocking animation and the timing of the attack. Practice blocking with your character's default blocking animation so that you are ready when Berthe starts to howl.

3. Use skills to enhance your block: Celestia has skills that can enhance your blocking ability, such as Parry and Knight's Valor. Use these skills to further improve your blocking ability and mitigate any damage from Berthe's howl.

4. Use immune frames: Some of Celestia's skills have invincibility frames, which means they provide temporary immunity from enemy attacks. Use these skills to avoid Berthe's howl, as it makes Celestia invulnerable for a brief period of time.

5. Position yourself carefully: Avoid standing too close to Berthe's front as it can trigger the howl attack. Instead, position yourself behind or to the side of Berthe, where it is less likely to howl.

6. Practice makes perfect: Blocking howls takes practice and patience. Keep practicing and refining your timing, positioning, and reflexes, and you will become better at blocking howls efficiently.

Remember, blocking howls in Berthe raid requires good timing, practice, and situational awareness. Be patient, stay focused, and work on improving your blocking skills, and you'll be able to overcome this challenge in no time.
Is Morpheus a good class dps wise?

Morpheus is a strong character in terms of DPS in the game Elsword. However, like all characters in the game, their effectiveness depends on your skill level and gear.

Morpheus has a high skill cap, meaning that mastering their skills and combos takes time and practice. However, once you have mastered their skills, they can deal significantly high damage to enemies.

Morpheus is also versatile in terms of their playstyle. They can deal both physical and magical damage, which can make it easier to deal with certain types of enemies or bosses. Additionally, Morpheus has skills that can immobilize or stun enemies, which can provide additional support to the team.

That being said, Morpheus may not be the best character for beginners, as they have a complex skill set and require good timing and precision to maximize their damage output. Furthermore, their gear requirements may be expensive, making it challenging to gear them up to their full potential.

In summary, Morpheus is a strong character in terms of DPS potential, but mastering their skills, timing, and gear requirements may take some time and practice.
Who is stronger - Liberator or Morpheus?

Both Liberator and Morpheus are strong characters in Elsword, but it's difficult to determine which one is "stronger" as they excel in different areas.

Liberator is a well-rounded character who can deal significant damage while also providing support to the team. They have a good mix of physical and magical damage, making them versatile against different types of enemies. Furthermore, they have skills that can grant buffs to the team, increase their damage output, and reduce the enemy's defenses.

Morpheus, on the other hand, is a high-damage character who can deal both physical and magical damage. They have a complex skill set that requires precise timing and good execution, but if used correctly, their skills can deal significant damage to enemies.

In terms of raw damage output, Morpheus has a higher potential, but they require good execution and gear to achieve their full potential. Liberator, on the other hand, may have slightly lower raw damage output but can provide significant support to the team, making them useful in group play.

Ultimately, the "strength" of a character depends on your skill level, gear, and personal playstyle. Both Liberator and Morpheus are considered strong characters in the game, but you may prefer one over the other based on your preferences and priorities.
How can Liberator support his team

Liberator can support his team in various ways. Here are some examples:

1. Holy Step: This skill not only deals damage but also leaves a Holy field on the ground which heals allies that stand inside it.

2. Noblese Oblige: This skill provides a large buff to the entire team, increasing both their physical and magical attack. The cooldown reduction from the buff also benefits the entire team.

3. Royal Blood: This skill regenerates HP for Liberator and nearby allies while also increasing their damage output. The duration of the buff increases with the number of enemies hit.

4. Sacred Blessing: This skill provides an invincibility bubble to all allies within range, preventing them from receiving damage for a short time.

5. Repentance: This skill removes negative status effects from allies within its range and provides a healing effect based on their current HP. Liberators can also use this skill to remove harmful debuffs from themselves.

6.
